title: 7 Herbal Creams For Hangover
intro_desc: Herbal creams for hangovers are becoming increasingly popular, and for good reason. When you're hungover, your body feels like it's been through a war – headaches, nausea, fatigue, and stomach issues are just a few symptoms you might be dealing with. Herbal teas, particularly those made with specific herbs, can help alleviate these symptoms.  Herbs like Zingiber officinale (ginger) are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe headaches and nausea. Curcuma longa (turmeric), another herb, has potent anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce swelling and ease pain. Ginkgo biloba, meanwhile, is often used to improve blood flow, which can help alleviate headaches and improve cognitive function.  These herbs work by targeting the root causes of hangover symptoms. For example, ginger's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce the swelling in your brain that's causing your headache. Similarly, turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds can help ease the pain and discomfort in your stomach. By using herbal teas or creams made with these herbs, you can begin to feel better more quickly and get back to your normal routine.  One of the benefits of using herbal creams for hangovers is that they're often more gentle on your body than traditional pain relief medications. While over-the-counter pain relievers can be effective, they can also have harsh side effects like stomach upset and drowsiness. Herbal creams, on the other hand, are often made with natural ingredients that are designed to work with your body, not against it.  In addition to being gentler on your body, herbal creams can also be more convenient than traditional pain relief methods. Instead of taking pills or drinking lots of water, you can simply apply a topical cream to your skin and let the healing properties work their magic. This can be especially helpful if you're not feeling up to drinking fluids or taking medication.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains the active constituents gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce nausea and vomiting, two common symptoms of a hangover. Gingerols and shogaols also have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the body from oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption. Additionally, the warming properties of ginger can help to increase blood flow and circulation, which can aid in the removal of toxins from the body. By reducing inflammation and promoting circulation, Zingiber officinale creams can help to alleviate some of the symptoms associated with a hangover.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for hangover
remedy_procedure: Mix 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, and 1/2 cup of beeswax in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring until the beeswax is melted.
Remove the saucepan from heat and add 2 tablespoons of Zingiber officinale essential oil, 2 tablespoons of peppermint essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of lavender essential oil.
Stir well and let the mixture cool until it reaches a temperature of around 100°F to 110°F.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains curcuminoids, specifically curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, which are its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce the severity of hangover symptoms. Curcumin specifically has been shown to block the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and molecules, which can contribute to hangover discomfort. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can exacerbate hangover symptoms. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Curcuma longa creams may help alleviate hangover symptoms such as headache, nausea, and fatigue.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for hangover
remedy_procedure: Mix 2 cups of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Melt the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Add 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder and 1 tablespoon of ginger powder to the melted mixture.
Stir well and add 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil and 1 teaspoon of peppermint oil.
Pour the mixture into a container, let it cool, and use as needed to relieve hangover symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavonoids and terpenoids as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce hangover symptoms. The flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba, specifically quercetin and kaempferol, work to alleviate oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption. The terpenoids, particularly bilobalide and ginkgolide, aid in improving blood circulation and reducing edema associated with hangovers. By addressing these underlying issues, Ginkgo biloba creams can provide relief from hangover symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for hangover
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove the melted mixture from the heat and stir in the Ginkgo biloba extract, vitamin E oil, and peppermint essential oil until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a small tin or jar and let it cool and solidify at room temperature.
Once solid, apply a small amount of the Ginkgo biloba cream to the affected area as needed to help alleviate hangover symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams contains compounds like quinine and cinchonine, which have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. These properties help reduce swelling and pain in the head, common symptoms of a hangover. Quinine also has a diuretic effect, which can help remove excess fluids and waste from the body, further alleviating hangover symptoms. Cinchonine has a sedative effect, which can help with sleep and relaxation, often disturbed by hangover-related discomfort. The antiviral properties of cinchona may also help combat the effects of dehydration and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal creams for hangover
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of glycerin, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Cinchona officinalis tincture, 10 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Melt beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add glycerin and distilled water to the melted beeswax. Stir until well combined.
Remove from heat and stir in Cinchona officinalis tincture and peppermint essential oil.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng creams contains active constituents like ginsenosides, particularly Rg1 and Rb1, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce the swelling and pain associated with a hangover. Ginsenosides also have antioxidant effects, which can neutralize free radicals in the body and alleviate oxidative stress caused by excessive drinking. The creams' anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties can help alleviate symptoms like headaches, fatigue, and nausea, making Panax ginseng creams a potential remedy for hangover relief. By applying the cream topically, the ginsenosides can be absorbed through the skin and directly into the bloodstream, providing fast and targeted relief.
remedies.image_alt: Panax ginseng herbal creams for hangover
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Panax ginseng extract, 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 teaspoons of aloe vera gel.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl in the microwave for 30-second intervals, stirring between each interval until smooth.
Add shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until combined. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Add Panax ginseng extract, vitamin E oil, and aloe vera gel to the cooled mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let cool completely before use. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams contains active constituents like rosmarinic acid, melissic acid, and ursolic acid, which are responsible for its hangover-relieving properties. Rosmarinic acid helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, while melissic acid and ursolic acid have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. These properties can help alleviate headaches, nausea, and fatigue associated with hangovers. Melissa officinalis creams also have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress caused by hangovers. The combination of these properties makes Melissa officinalis creams a potentially effective remedy for hangover relief.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for hangover
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Melissa officinalis flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of essential oil (optional).
Combine 1 cup of Melissa officinalis flowers and 1 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat for 2-3 hours, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, discarding the flowers. Add 1/2 cup of beeswax to the mixture and heat it until the beeswax melts.
Remove the mixture from heat and add 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil and 2 tablespoons of essential oil (if using). Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, your Melissa officinalis cream is ready to use for hangover relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains rosmarinic acid, a powerful antioxidant that helps reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to hangover symptoms. This acid also has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate headaches and fatigue associated with hangovers. Additionally, the cream's high levels of carnosic acid, a potent antioxidant, help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by excessive drinking. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Rosmarinus officinalis creams may also help soothe digestive issues and reduce nausea, common hangover complaints. By applying Rosmarinus officinalis creams topically, the body may be able to absorb these beneficial compounds and start to recover from a hangover more quickly.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for hangover
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, 2 tablespoons of rosemary essential oil, and a few drops of peppermint essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Stir in vitamin E oil and rosemary essential oil. Add a few drops of peppermint essential oil for a refreshing scent.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, your rosemary hangover cream is ready to use. Apply to affected areas as needed.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help ease hangover symptoms, but it's not a guaranteed prevention method. Some herbs like peppermint and ginger can calm stomach issues and reduce nausea. However, the effects of herbal tea on hangovers are largely anecdotal and not extensively scientifically proven.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for hangovers daily isn't recommended. While they may help alleviate symptoms temporarily, relying on them daily can lead to caffeine overload and interact with other medications. Herbal teas also have varying levels of quality and potential side effects. It's best to use them occasionally and focus on a balanced lifestyle to avoid hangovers.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with hangover symptoms, but the time it takes to feel better varies. Some people report relief within 30 minutes to an hour after drinking, while others may take a few hours. The effectiveness also depends on the type of herbal tea and the severity of the hangover.
faq_time: For hangover relief, drink herbal tea when your body needs a boost, usually in the morning. This is when your body is trying to recover from the night's activities. A warm cup of herbal tea can help ease stomach discomfort and replenish lost fluids, giving you a gentle start to the day.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas may interact with some prescription medications for hangover, such as blood thinners or diabetes meds. This can happen if the tea affects how the body processes these medications, making them stronger or weaker than intended. If you're taking prescription meds, it's a good idea to check the ingredients of the herbal tea you're drinking.
faq_pregnant: While some herbal teas can be soothing for hangovers, it's best to be cautious when pregnant. Some teas, like those with caffeine or certain herbs, might not be good for your baby's development. Stick to gentle options like peppermint or chamomile, but always check the label for any added ingredients or warnings.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for hangovers can be a tempting solution while breastfeeding. However, some herbs like peppermint and chamomile are safe, but others like sage and pennyroyal may cause issues. It's best to stick with gentle, well-known herbs and check the ingredients to avoid any potential risks.
title_2: The Hangover Fix: 7 Herbal Creams for Social Drinkers
